wizard.introLine1: Looki pulls events and forwards them to an OpenClaw Agent.
wizard.introLine2: This wizard configures language, baseUrl, apiKey, and optional forwarding.

language.title: Language
language.message: Choose the interface language (only for this wizard, not saved)
language.saved: Saved interface language for next run: {locale}

env.title: Environment
env.note: Step 1: choose the deployment environment. This controls Looki baseUrl.
env.customMessage: Enter custom Looki baseUrl (starting with http:// or https://)
env.customInvalid: Please enter a valid http(s) URL

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.note: Step 2: enter the Looki API key.

plugin.title: Plugin Detection
plugin.none: No forwarding plugins were detected. Only the basic Looki config will be saved.
plugin.detectedNoSessions: Detected {labels}, but none of them have any existing chat sessions yet. Have the target user message the bot once, then rerun the wizard.

listControls.title: Hint
listControls.body: Enter to configure · Esc to exit

session.message: Pick a {label} session to forward to

forward.targetMessage: Choose chat plugins for Agent output forwarding

openclaw.missing: OpenClaw was not found. Please install it first:

version.missing: Could not detect the OpenClaw version. Please make sure `openclaw --version` works.

install.failedManual: Plugin install failed. Please run it manually:
install.updateFailedManual: Plugin update failed. Please run it manually:

restart.failedManual: Restart failed. You can run this manually:

help.install: install           Install the Looki plugin and complete basic setup
help.configure: configure         Re-run the configuration wizard without installing
help.optionLocale: --locale <code>   Force interface language (en | es | fr | ja | zh-CN)
help.optionNoRestart: --no-restart      Skip `openclaw gateway restart` after writing config

diagnose.openclawMissing: Hint: `openclaw` is not on PATH. Try `npm install -g openclaw` and reopen the shell.
diagnose.eacces: Hint: looks like a permission error. If you installed openclaw with `sudo npm install -g`, re-try with your user's npm prefix or fix npm's global permissions.
diagnose.enoent: Hint: the target binary or path was not found. Verify the install completed successfully.
diagnose.network: Hint: looks like a network issue. Check outbound connectivity to npm and the OpenClaw gateway host.
diagnose.configReadFail: Hint: failed to read {path}. Check file permissions; the CLI needs read+write access to `~/.openclaw/`.
